failure_surface: Agent attempted to convert all cell values in numeric-looking columns directly to integers without checking for non-numeric string values (e.g., '>48'), causing ValueError crash during header parsing.
root_cause: The agent assumed all cells in the month header row contained numeric values that could be safely converted with int(). When encountering a string value prefixed with '>' (representing a threshold condition), the direct int() conversion failed catastrophically, halting the entire workflow.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before converting cell values to numeric types, the code should inspect the actual Python type and handle string values specially. Implement try-except wrapping around type conversions with fallback handling for non-numeric formats.
evidence: First execution crashed with: ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: '>48'
Data sheet row 2 contains mixed numeric headers (1,2,3...) and string values like '>48' representing month thresholds
Third code iteration fixed the issue by adding isinstance checks and tuple storage for '>N' formatted values

memory_items.title: Validate Cell Types Before Type Conversion
memory_items.lesson: Never assume cell values match expected types even in seemingly numeric columns. Always check isinstance(value, (int, float)) or use try-except patterns.
memory_items.generalization: When reading spreadsheets with openpyxl, verify the actual Python type of each cell value before applying int(), float(), or other type coercions.

memory_items.title: Handle Special String Encodings in Numeric Data
memory_items.lesson: Numeric columns may contain special markers (e.g., '>48', '<=12') that represent conditional ranges rather than plain numbers.
memory_items.generalization: Detect and preserve special prefix/suffix notation in numeric columns; store parsed intent (comparison operator + value) rather than forcing pure numeric conversion.

memory_items.title: Defensive Parsing Prevents Catastrophic Failures
memory_items.lesson: Wrap data loading loops with error handling so one malformed cell doesn't crash the entire operation.
memory_items.generalization: Use try-except blocks around parsing logic and implement graceful degradation (skip, mark as NaN, log warning) when encountering unexpected cell formats.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Handling Mixed-Type Columns
- **Validation First**: Never assume cell values match expected types even in numeric columns. Check `isinstance(cell_value, (int, float))` before conversion.
- **Special Formats**: Numeric columns may contain threshold notation (e.g., '>48', '<=12') representing conditions rather than literals. Detect prefixes and store parsed intent as structured data.
- **Error Resilience**: Wrap cell parsing in try-except blocks; degrade gracefully (skip, mark NaN, continue) rather than crashing on single-cell anomalies.
